question.text: Which of the following is a mandatory duty of every Regulated Entity under the IFSCA (AML, CFT and KYC) Guidelines, 2022?

answer.text: Formulate an AML-CFT policy approved by the Governing Body or a committee delegated by the Governing Body
Formulate an AML-CFT policy approved only by the Principal Officer
Adopt the standard AML-CFT policy template issued by IFSCA without any modification
Formulate an AML-CFT policy approved by the FIU-IND Director

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Every Regulated Entity shall formulate an AML-CFT policy which shall be duly approved by the Governing Body or by a committee to whom such power has been delegated by the Governing Body. The KYC Policy shall be part of the AML-CFT policy.
**The Trap:** The approval must come from the Governing Body (board-level), not just the Principal Officer or compliance team. This ensures board-level oversight of AML/CFT matters.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Similar to Series XXIV where SEBI mandates board-approved AML policies. In IFSCA-01, the term 'Governing Body' is used instead of 'Board of Directors' to accommodate various entity structures in IFSC.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Governing Body approval requirement ensures top-down accountability. Every member of Senior Management is personally responsible for compliance — not just the compliance officer.
